D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Lateral Displacement through a Glass Plate

send a ray through a glass slab and measure how far it shifts sideways on exit

A ray entering a parallel-faced slab refracts twice and exits parallel to its original direction but laterally displaced.

Slide angle, slab thickness, and index. The crimson segment at the exit plane measures the displacement d; the dashed line shows where the ray would have gone without the slab.
